log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a UPX-compressed PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader' and 'nxdomain'. It's 57KB in size. Now I need to gather more IOC data to understand the full scope of the threat. I'll call get_al

The IOC summary confirms 1 unique domain (mokoshoponline.com), 1 dropped file, and 1 process created. No persistence keys or mutexes of interest were found (the mutexes seen earlier were system mutexe

The network activity shows the malware attempts to download 'gifts_card.exe' from mokoshoponline.com, which is a suspicious domain name mimicking a legitimate store. The DNS resolution failed (empty I

The dropped file 'hhcbrnaff.exe' is the unpacked payload from the UPX-compressed initial sample. The name looks random but could be a legitimate tool name (hhcbrnaff.exe doesn't immediately ring alarm

The process activity shows the dropped file was executed from the Temp folder. The notable modules include standard Windows libraries but also some audio-related ones (mciwave.dll, midimap.dll, wdmaud

The registry changes show no persistence keys, which is interesting given the initial report mentioned some registry keys being set (MUICache and Internet Settings). These appear to be artifacts of th
